data.short_description: Supper club-style restaurant with vintage-inspired design and mezzanine lounge. Menu features contemporary American dishes including steaks, pork chops, short ribs, and house-made desserts. Owned by Malachy Noone with partners Kyle Miller and Frederick Hancock.

A 2,700 sq ft restaurant with ~66 seats and hybrid counter/table service. The first North Carolina location for the brand, featuring brisket, pulled pork, spare ribs, smoked chicken, and Carolina vinegar-style barbecue. Owned by Abby Haywood and Evangeline Smith.

Speakeasy-style bourbon bar and cigar lounge with hidden entrance and upscale interior. Menu highlights include bourbon-focused cocktails, Neapolitan pizza, and small plates. Owned by Ralph Mensah and Michael Thomas.

Coffee shop, bookstore, and vinyl concept with rotating curated inventory and workspace vibe. Menu includes a coffee program plus curated books and records with a local focus. Owned by Jermaine Bantum.
Restaurant targeting a summer opening at 318 Blackwell St, Durham. Listed as being in early construction phase. Article text is truncated; no further details available.

Fast-casual barbecue restaurant with a drive-thru and limited indoor seating for about 12 guests, opening in the former Mr. K's BBQ space. Menu centers on pit-cooked barbecue including chicken, ribs, and classic sides. Owned by Robert Rukab and partner Zee.

data.location: 6290 N Atlantic Ave, Cape Canaveral, FL 32920
data.short_description: Cozy, beach-inspired café with indoor seating and a patio near the cruise port. Menu features espresso drinks, baked goods, and the Dawn Patrol Latte. Owned by Sofia Ross and Brayden Montgomery.

data.location: 4285 N Atlantic Ave, Cocoa Beach, FL 32931
data.short_description: Fast-casual smoothie shop connected to neighboring concepts. Menu includes no-ice smoothies, fresh juices, and açaí bowls. Owned by Ron Abeles.

data.location: 174 Canaveral Plaza Blvd., Cocoa Beach, FL 32931
data.short_description: Family-run coffee and ice cream shop with counter service. Menu includes ice cream, shakes, Italian sodas, and specialty coffee. Owned by Alyssa and Travis Froland.

data.location: 820 E New Haven Ave, Suite 3, Melbourne, FL 32901
data.short_description: Hybrid tea service concept with Victorian and boho design. Menu includes loose leaf teas, sandwiches, soups, and baked goods. Owned by Noah Nace.

data.short_description: Quick-service cookie shop with a service window and outdoor seating, featuring an open kitchen concept. Menu includes a rotating monthly selection of cookies, milkshakes, coffee, cookie sandwiches, and catering options. Owned by classically trained chef Brisma Fermin.

data.location: 2110 Powers Ferry Rd Suite 120, Atlanta, GA 30339
data.short_description: First permanent brick-and-mortar location for the vegan concept, transitioning from food truck and pop-up operations. The café seats up to 54 guests indoors plus an outdoor patio, serving vegan Philly, tacos, burgers, and other dishes. Owned by mother-daughter team Tanetta Ellzey and Iyonna Poags.
Craft brewery targeting an April 1 opening in Southport, NC. Led by partners Alan A. McDonald, Kevin J. Wright, and Kevin Bower.

The location features walk-up window ordering and outdoor picnic tables. Founded by Daniel Ray, who won the Atlanta Grilled Cheese Festival in 2019.

data.location: 2960 Ball Ground/Canton Hwy, Ball Ground, GA 30107
data.short_description: New food truck concept focused on steak and frites, primarily serving hanger steaks over fries with sauces including chimichurri. Will be stationed at the same Ball Ground location as Cheez'd and Confuzed. Owned by Daniel Ray.

Second Potbelly location for owners Patrick and Leah Pendergast in the Jacksonville market. Expected to have about 20 indoor seats plus a few patio tables, part of a seven-location Northeast Florida growth plan.
First Potbelly location for owners Patrick and Leah Pendergast in the Jacksonville market, debuting in April. Part of a larger seven-location Northeast Florida expansion plan.

data.location: 6745 Land O' Lakes Blvd, Land O' Lakes, FL 34638
data.short_description: Compact dessert shop with limited indoor seating and additional outdoor seating, featuring about 60 homemade ice cream flavors daily plus cookies, brownies, cake pops, and custom ice cream cakes. Counter ordering via digital menu. Owned by Michelle Sorensen and George Stacy.

data.location: 8528 Ridge Rd, New Port Richey, FL 34654
data.short_description: Takeout-focused Asian fusion concept with limited dine-in seating for about 20–30 guests and a drive-thru pickup lane for online orders. Menu features bento-style meals, ramen, noodle dishes, wings, soups, and seasonal selections. Owned by Yunzhong Chen.

data.location: 6130 Massachusetts Ave., New Port Richey, FL 34653
data.short_description: Fast-casual coffee shop designed as a gathering space with about 15 indoor seats and roughly 25 outdoor seats in a landscaped garden setting. Article text is truncated; full menu details unavailable.

Small fast-casual bagel shop near the south end of Siesta Key with limited indoor and shared outdoor courtyard seating. Menu features fresh daily New York-style bagels with house-made cream cheeses, salmon toppings, espresso drinks, and canned-to-order cold brew. Owned by restaurateur Robert DeSilvio.

Revival of a nostalgic Sarasota drive-in concept with counter ordering and kiosks supporting dine-in, takeout, delivery, and catering options. Menu features classic burgers, fries, onion rings, and milkshakes. Majority owned by Troy King with partner Dimitris Syros.

Full-service Japanese restaurant paired with a cocktail lounge inside a restored historic building, with initial seating for roughly 135 guests. Menu includes vegan sushi, tableside hibachi, filet-focused steak options, pineapple fried rice, and Korean short rib. Owned by Samuel Ray with partners Jeffrey Karasawa and Jason Klamson.

Slider concept operating from the brand's signature bright orange shipping-container "can" with patio seating. Menu features cheeseburger sliders, seasoned waffle fries, and shakes. Franchisee Rocky Moore and team.

Counter-service Italian deli and market spanning about 1,800 square feet offering hot and cold sandwiches, deli salads, desserts, grab-and-go meals, and beer and wine. Targeting a May opening as final equipment installation and setup continue. Owned by Brandon Brock.

Craft brewery combining two buildings into approximately 2,000 square feet indoors with an outdoor beer garden featuring a live music stage, on a quarter-acre property.
